title: Hymenosicyos bequaertii
intro: Hymenosicyos bequaertii, also known as Bequaert's milktree or Philippine sand vine, is a type of plant in the Cucurbitaceae family, characterized by its milky sap and woody climber growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Bioactive constituents of Hymenosicyos bequaertii include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions, as well as a tea for fever and digestive issues.
